exemplar.title: Deriving and Applying the Surface Area of Revolution Formula to Estimate Volume of Paint Required for a Vase

detailedExemplarData.title: Deriving and Applying the Surface Area of Revolution Formula to Estimate Volume of Paint Required for a Vase

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Logical narrative with clear introduction, development and conclusion
Effective use of headings, numbered sections and labelled figures
Descriptive title framing the investigationâs focus
weaknesses: Verbose passages with lengthy digressions interrupt coherence
Occasional algebraic slips and minor overlaps between sections
Tables lacking captions and explicit column headings hinder clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration is coherent and logically developed, with a well-structured organization using clear sections and figures. However, extensive recapitulations and occasional algebraic errors reduce conciseness, restricting the work to a solid Band 3 rather than the highest level.

strengths: Appropriate and mostly consistent use of integral, derivative and function notation
Good use of multiple representations (equations, tables, GeoGebra diagrams, piecewise graphs)
Clear logical thread in explanations and modelling decisions
weaknesses: Minor lapses in notation (missing dx, inconsistent function casing)
Axis labels and scale indicators on diagrams are sometimes too small
Typographical errors in complex fractions and unexplained calculator outputs reduce clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical language and notation are used with good consistency, and multiple representations effectively support the text. Explanations follow a clear logical order, but minor notational inconsistencies and typographical errors prevent a Band 4 award, placing this criterion firmly in Band 3.

strengths: Evidence of independent decision-making in choosing the artefact and deriving a non-syllabus formula
A clearly personal modelling approach, including hand-selecting regions and adjusting domains
Creative use of interpolation and technology to manage complex coefficients
weaknesses: Once the approach is set, the work follows standard calculus procedures
No particularly novel mathematical insight or advanced optimisation
Personal engagement is solid but not sustained at an exceptional level
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration shows significant independent thinking and a clear personal approach, with creative initiative in choosing methods. Yet it remains within the bounds of standard IA techniques, meriting a Band 2 score.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on measurement uncertainty, decimal-place choices and human error
Thoughtful analysis of process choices and their impact on accuracy
Clear evaluation of outcomes with suggestions for technological improvements
weaknesses: Lacks quantitative error analysis to deepen reflection
Reflections remain at a general level without alternative model testing
Evaluation does not include a comprehensive plan for future investigation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student provides consistent, meaningful reflection and thoughtful analysis of results, with clear evaluation of implications. However, absence of deeper quantitative critique and alternative modelling keeps this within Band 2.

strengths: Comprehensive application of relevant mathematics including non-syllabus surface-area integrals and piecewise polynomial modelling
Mathematics fully commensurate with course level and expertly integrated into the real-world context
Thorough understanding demonstrated by correct summation of areas and conversion to volume
weaknesses: Minor typographical and algebraic inaccuracies in formula derivations
Overcomplication in some derivations (e.g. invoking Mean Value Theorem)
Occasional inconsistency in unit presentation and significant-figure usage
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration employs a broad and appropriate range of advanced mathematical techniques, all applied expertly to solve the stated problem. Despite a few technical slips and presentation details, the depth, accuracy and sophistication of the mathematics justify the maximum Band 6 for Use of Mathematics.

strandSelections.reasoning: Explanations accompany each algebraic or technological step, and justifications for modelling decisions are given, giving the write-up a solid logical thread. Nevertheless, typographical errors within long fractions and a few unexplained calculator outputs lessen absolute clarity, positioning the work in the âgoodâ band.

The student shows good conceptual grasp, correctly differentiating and setting up integrals. Yet there are technical inaccuracies: the derived general formula contains typographical errors, and the final numerical area is presented without verification (units and magnitude could be cross-checked). Hence understanding is solid with minor errorsâband 4.

The student employs equations, tables of coordinates, multiple GeoGebra screenshots and a final piecewise graph, allowing the mathematics to be viewed in several ways. These representations are generally clear and appropriately linked to the text, although axis labels and scale indicators are sometimes small, so effectiveness is good but not outstanding.

The report follows a logical narrative: introduction, derivation of a non-syllabus formula, modelling, calculation, evaluation and conclusion. Arguments generally flow from purpose to method to result, so a reader can follow the thinking without getting lost. However, frequent digressions (e.g., lengthy recaps of Riemann sums and Mean Value Theorem) interrupt the line of reasoning and a few algebraic slips break smooth progression, preventing an âexcellentâ descriptor.

Employing Lagrange interpolation, piecewise fitting, and technology to handle unwieldy coefficients reflects creativity and initiative. However, these techniques are standard tools in IA work; no particularly novel mathematical insight or sophisticated optimisation is pursued, so the work sits comfortably in the âgoodâ band.

The student comments on the effect of scaling errors, choice of points and domain adjustments on accuracy, showing thoughtful analysis of process and results. The critique remains fairly general; no quantitative error analysis or alternative modelling is attempted, hence the work fits the âthoughtfulâ tier.

Section 7 evaluates outcomes, noting how the calculated volume may misestimate due to dripping and surface irregularities, and proposes technological improvements. This is a clear evaluation with some implications, but it lacks a comprehensive, data-backed assessment or future testing plan, so the mid-band is appropriate.

Reflection is woven through Section 7, discussing measurement uncertainty, choice of decimal places and potential human error, which constitutes meaningful reflection. The observations are sensible but not deeply probing or iterative, so the strand meets, but does not exceed, the âmeaningfulâ descriptor.

Notation for integrals, derivatives and functions is appropriate and usually consistent; Ï, Îx and prime notation are used correctly. Minor lapses such as mixing upper- and lower-case for functions and an occasional missing dx keep the work at âmostly consistentâ rather than fully consistent.

The modelling processâoverlaying an image, hand-selecting regions, and adjusting domainsâdemonstrates a personal route to the solution. The approach is clearly the studentâs own, yet it does not rise to the level of being highly innovative or idiosyncratic throughout.

Most content relates directly to estimating paint volume, but the narrative is verboseâseveral pages are spent re-deriving standard results or quoting theorems with little bearing on the final answer. Occasional repetition of measurements and prolonged decimal-place discussion reduce overall conciseness, hence only âmostly relevantâ.

The mathematicsâpiecewise polynomial modelling, non-syllabus surface-area integral, and numerical evaluationâcovers a broad range and is applied directly to solve the stated problem. The work is more than merely appropriate; it is comprehensive, addressing derivation, modelling and application, though not flawless enough for the top band.

Choosing a personal artefact, deciding to derive a non-syllabus formula, and segmenting the profile show clear independent choices beyond textbook examples. Still, the exploration largely follows standard calculus procedures once the decisions are made, so evidence is significant but not outstanding.

Headings, numbered sections and labelled figures give the study a clear structure. Each major step (data collection, model building, integration, evaluation) sits in its own subsection, aiding navigation. Minor overlaps between sections 4 and 5, and some tables inserted without captions, show that organisation is good rather than exemplary.

Techniques used (Lagrange interpolation, calculus of surfaces of revolution, numerical integration on GDC) are fully commensurate with HL level and are well-applied to a real-life context. Minor execution slips keep the strand from âexpertlyâ but merit the âwell-appliedâ 5-mark band.

annotations.comment: The title is descriptive and clearly frames the investigationâs focus on surface area and paint volume, engaging the reader effectively.
The introduction outlines the aim but could more succinctly state the research question in a single, clear sentence to improve focus.
The explanation of deriving the volume formula could be strengthened by explicitly linking each Riemann sum step to the integral form and clarifying any omitted differentials.
Clarify the contrast between approximating volume with thin cylinders and surface area with frustums; the current discussion conflates these methods and may confuse readers.
The GeoGebra diagram is clear but lacks axis labels and scale markers, hindering full interpretation of the frustum modelâs dimensions.
The variable Îy_f is introduced but not defined in terms of f(x); include its analytic expression to aid clarity.
Equation for A_s (block starting 187.66 â 542.57) contains mismatched parentheses and missing operators; revise to match standard form $$A_s=Ï[f(x_{f-1})+f(x_f)]\,l$$.
The Mean Value Theorem is invoked to justify the slant height formula, but this step overcomplicates the derivation. Directly using $$l=â{1+(f'(x_i))^2}\,Îx$$ suffices.
The notation $$x_o$$ appears without definition. Ensure each new symbol is defined before use to maintain mathematical clarity.
Subscripts in the limit expression $$f'(x_a)$$ should consistently use $$f'(x_i)$$ or simply $$f'(x)$$ as $$Îxâ0$$ to avoid confusion.
Reflection on measurement uncertainty is noted later, but include a quantified error analysis here when describing how circumference was measured.
Converting circumference to radius uses 2 s.f. but earlier measurements used 3 s.f., resulting in inconsistent precision. Align significant figures across calculations.
The polynomial regression description mentions degree nine but shows all zero coefficients for higher terms. Clarify why those terms vanish or choose a lower-degree fit.
Table 1 lists coordinates but lacks clear x- and y-column headings, making interpretation difficult. Label each column explicitly.
The regional division diagram is effective but would benefit from clear domain labels on the dividing lines for immediate reference.
The unsimplified Lagrange expansion is overwhelming. Present only the final simplified polynomial to improve readability and mathematical simplicity.
Region Bâs Lagrange polynomial shows a placeholder coefficient â0x4â that appears incorrect. Verify the interpolation inputs and recalculate.
The x-values in the Region B interpolation include 14 despite the point I having x=12. Check point selection consistency.
Region Dâs function is labelled $$P_a(x)$$ instead of $$P_d(x)$$. Consistent notation for each piece avoids confusion.
The heading for Region C uses $$P_d(x)$$ in the equation but should be $$P_c(x)$$ to align with earlier naming conventions.
The regional cubic function is later defined as $$R(x)$$ but originally as $$P_c(x)$$. Retain one symbol for each region to maintain clarity.
Three roots are found for the intersection equation, but the extraneous root should be discarded with justification in the context of the vaseâs domain.

The piecewise definition of $$g(x)$$ omits conventional function-value notation. Use a format like $$g(x)=

 egin{cases}â¦

ight.$$ for clarity.
The computed value $$A_a=170.92$$ is given without units in the result. Append âcmÂ²â to all numerical surface-area outcomes.
The summation of regional areas into $$A_{vase}=7637.56	ext{ cm}^2$$ is correct and clearly presented, demonstrating strong integration of results.
The conversion to paint volume uses the ratio 1 ml per 4 cmÂ² correctly, yielding 1909.39 ml and demonstrating appropriate application of units.
The evaluation section acknowledges measurement and plotting uncertainties, providing meaningful reflection on potential sources of error.
The extension proposes optimizing vase design via constrained calculus, showing forward-looking engagement with real-world applications.
